#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_act_tests.sh - Test harness that runs the GitHub Actions workflow via act
#
# For each test case, this harness:
#   1. Creates a temporary git repo with the project files and that case's fixture
#   2. Runs `act push --rm` against the workflow
#   3. Captures output to act-result.txt (appended, clearly delimited)
#   4. Asserts exact expected values appear in the output
#   5. Asserts every job shows "Job succeeded"
#
# Additionally verifies:
#   - Workflow YAML structure (triggers, jobs, steps)
#   - File references in the workflow are valid
#   - actionlint passes

set -euo pipefail

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Configuration
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
ACT_RESULT="${SCRIPT_DIR}/act-result.txt"
PLATFORM="ubuntu-latest=catthehacker/ubuntu:act-latest"
WORKFLOW_FILE=".github/workflows/artifact-cleanup-script.yml"

# Clear results file
: > "$ACT_RESULT"

PASS_COUNT=0
FAIL_COUNT=0

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
log()  { echo "[INFO]  $*"; }
pass() { echo "[PASS]  $*"; PASS_COUNT=$(( PASS_COUNT + 1 )); }
fail() { echo "[FAIL]  $*" >&2; FAIL_COUNT=$(( FAIL_COUNT + 1 )); }

# assert_contains LABEL EXPECTED FILE
# Checks that FILE contains the EXPECTED string; records pass/fail.
assert_contains() {
    local label="$1" expected="$2" file="$3"
    if grep -qF "$expected" "$file"; then
        pass "$label: found '$expected'"
    else
        fail "$label: expected '$expected' not found in output"
    fi
}

# assert_not_contains LABEL UNEXPECTED FILE
assert_not_contains() {
    local label="$1" unexpected="$2" file="$3"
    if grep -qF "$unexpected" "$file"; then
        fail "$label: unexpected '$unexpected' found in output"
    else
        pass "$label: correctly absent '$unexpected'"
    fi
}

# run_act_for_test_case LABEL TC_ID FIXTURE_SRC EXTRA_ENV...
# Creates an isolated temp git repo, copies project files + the given fixture
# as tests/fixtures/<name>.csv, runs act, appends to act-result.txt, and
# returns the path to the per-case log file.
run_act_for_test_case() {
    local label="$1"
    local tc_id="$2"
    local fixture_src="$3"
    shift 3
    local extra_env=("$@")  # array of "--env KEY=VAL" pairs

    local tmpdir
    tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
    # Ensure cleanup even on failure
    # shellcheck disable=SC2064
    trap "rm -rf '$tmpdir'" EXIT INT TERM

    log "Setting up temp repo for $label in $tmpdir"

    # --- Copy project files into temp repo ---
    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/artifact_cleanup.sh" "$tmpdir/"
    mkdir -p "$tmpdir/tests/fixtures"
    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/artifact_cleanup.bats" "$tmpdir/tests/"
    # Copy all fixture files so bats tests can find them
    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/"*.csv "$tmpdir/tests/fixtures/"
    # Copy the test-case-specific fixture as the canonical one too
    cp "$fixture_src" "$tmpdir/tests/fixtures/current_fixture.csv"
    mkdir -p "$tmpdir/.github/workflows"
    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE" "$tmpdir/$WORKFLOW_FILE"

    # --- Initialise git repo (actions/checkout@v4 needs a git history) ---
    git -C "$tmpdir" init -q
    git -C "$tmpdir" config user.email "test@example.com"
    git -C "$tmpdir" config user.name "Test"
    git -C "$tmpdir" add -A
    git -C "$tmpdir" commit -qm "test: $label"

    # --- Build act command ---
    local act_cmd=(
        act push
        --rm
        --platform "$PLATFORM"
        --env "TC_ID=$tc_id"
        --env "REFDATE=9999999999"
        --no-cache-server
        --directory "$tmpdir"
    )
    for env_pair in "${extra_env[@]+"${extra_env[@]}"}"; do
        act_cmd+=(--env "$env_pair")
    done

    local case_log="$tmpdir/act_output.txt"

    log "Running act for $label (TC_ID=$tc_id)..."
    set +e
    "${act_cmd[@]}" 2>&1 | tee "$case_log"
    local act_exit=$?
    set -e

    # --- Append to global act-result.txt with clear delimiter ---
    {
        echo ""
        echo "################################################################"
        echo "# TEST CASE: $label (TC_ID=$tc_id)"
        echo "# act exit code: $act_exit"
        echo "################################################################"
        cat "$case_log"
        echo "################################################################"
        echo "# END: $label"
        echo "################################################################"
    } >> "$ACT_RESULT"

    # --- Assert act exit code ---
    if [[ "$act_exit" -eq 0 ]]; then
        pass "$label: act exited 0"
    else
        fail "$label: act exited $act_exit (non-zero)"
    fi

    echo "$case_log"  # return log path for caller to assert against
    # Reset the EXIT trap before returning so the caller's temp dir isn't removed
    trap - EXIT INT TERM
    # The tmpdir will be cleaned up naturally when the subshell exits;
    # we keep it alive until the caller is done asserting.
    export LAST_CASE_TMPDIR="$tmpdir"
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 0: Workflow structure and static checks
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " SECTION 0: Workflow Structure & Static Validation"
echo "======================================================="

{
    echo ""
    echo "################################################################"
    echo "# SECTION 0: Workflow Structure & Static Validation"
    echo "################################################################"
} >> "$ACT_RESULT"

# Check that workflow file exists
if [[ -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE" ]]; then
    pass "Workflow file exists at $WORKFLOW_FILE"
    echo "STRUCTURE: workflow file exists at $WORKFLOW_FILE" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
else
    fail "Workflow file missing: $WORKFLOW_FILE"
fi

# Verify workflow references correct script path
if grep -qF "artifact_cleanup.sh" "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE"; then
    pass "Workflow references artifact_cleanup.sh"
    echo "STRUCTURE: workflow references artifact_cleanup.sh" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
else
    fail "Workflow does not reference artifact_cleanup.sh"
fi

# Verify script file exists
if [[ -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/artifact_cleanup.sh" ]]; then
    pass "Script file artifact_cleanup.sh exists"
else
    fail "Script file artifact_cleanup.sh missing"
fi

# Verify fixture files referenced in workflow exist
for fixture in test_max_age test_keep_latest test_max_size test_combined test_dry_run; do
    fixture_path="$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/${fixture}.csv"
    if [[ -f "$fixture_path" ]]; then
        pass "Fixture exists: tests/fixtures/${fixture}.csv"
    else
        fail "Fixture missing: tests/fixtures/${fixture}.csv"
    fi
done

# Verify actionlint passes
log "Running actionlint..."
actionlint_output=$(actionlint "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE" 2>&1) && actionlint_exit=0 || actionlint_exit=$?
echo "ACTIONLINT EXIT: $actionlint_exit" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
echo "$actionlint_output" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
if [[ "$actionlint_exit" -eq 0 ]]; then
    pass "actionlint: workflow passes"
else
    fail "actionlint: workflow has errors: $actionlint_output"
fi

# Verify workflow has expected trigger events
for trigger in push pull_request workflow_dispatch schedule; do
    if grep -qF "$trigger" "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE"; then
        pass "Workflow has trigger: $trigger"
    else
        fail "Workflow missing trigger: $trigger"
    fi
done

# Verify expected job names exist in workflow
for job in lint unit-tests integration; do
    if grep -qF "$job" "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE"; then
        pass "Workflow has job: $job"
    else
        fail "Workflow missing job: $job"
    fi
done

# Static analysis on the main script
if shellcheck "$SCRIPT_DIR/artifact_cleanup.sh" 2>/dev/null; then
    pass "shellcheck: artifact_cleanup.sh passes"
    echo "SHELLCHECK: passed" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
else
    fail "shellcheck: artifact_cleanup.sh has warnings"
fi

if bash -n "$SCRIPT_DIR/artifact_cleanup.sh" 2>/dev/null; then
    pass "bash -n: artifact_cleanup.sh syntax valid"
else
    fail "bash -n: artifact_cleanup.sh syntax error"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 1: Test Case 1 — max-age policy
# Expected: DELETE old-artifact, RETAIN new-artifact; space_reclaimed=1048576
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " SECTION 1: TC1 — max-age policy"
echo "======================================================="

tc1_log=$(run_act_for_test_case \
    "TC1: max-age policy" \
    "tc1" \
    "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/test_max_age.csv")

assert_contains "TC1" "DELETE old-artifact max_age 1048576" "$tc1_log"
assert_contains "TC1" "RETAIN new-artifact"                  "$tc1_log"
assert_contains "TC1" "space_reclaimed=1048576"              "$tc1_log"
assert_contains "TC1" "total=2"                              "$tc1_log"
assert_contains "TC1" "retained=1"                           "$tc1_log"
assert_contains "TC1" "deleted=1"                            "$tc1_log"
assert_contains "TC1" "Job succeeded"                        "$tc1_log"
rm -rf "$LAST_CASE_TMPDIR"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 2: Test Case 2 — keep-latest-N policy
# Expected: DELETE artifact-v1 (oldest), RETAIN artifact-v2 artifact-v3
# space_reclaimed=524288
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " SECTION 2: TC2 — keep-latest-N policy"
echo "======================================================="

tc2_log=$(run_act_for_test_case \
    "TC2: keep-latest-N policy" \
    "tc2" \
    "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/test_keep_latest.csv")

assert_contains "TC2" "DELETE artifact-v1 keep_latest 524288" "$tc2_log"
assert_contains "TC2" "RETAIN artifact-v2"                     "$tc2_log"
assert_contains "TC2" "RETAIN artifact-v3"                     "$tc2_log"
assert_contains "TC2" "space_reclaimed=524288"                 "$tc2_log"
assert_contains "TC2" "total=3"                                "$tc2_log"
assert_contains "TC2" "retained=2"                             "$tc2_log"
assert_contains "TC2" "deleted=1"                              "$tc2_log"
assert_contains "TC2" "Job succeeded"                          "$tc2_log"
rm -rf "$LAST_CASE_TMPDIR"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 3: Test Case 3 — max-total-size policy
# Expected: DELETE artifact-a (oldest 3 MB), RETAIN artifact-b artifact-c
# space_reclaimed=3145728
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " SECTION 3: TC3 — max-total-size policy"
echo "======================================================="

tc3_log=$(run_act_for_test_case \
    "TC3: max-total-size policy" \
    "tc3" \
    "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/test_max_size.csv")

assert_contains "TC3" "DELETE artifact-a max_total_size 3145728" "$tc3_log"
assert_contains "TC3" "RETAIN artifact-b"                         "$tc3_log"
assert_contains "TC3" "RETAIN artifact-c"                         "$tc3_log"
assert_contains "TC3" "space_reclaimed=3145728"                   "$tc3_log"
assert_contains "TC3" "total=3"                                   "$tc3_log"
assert_contains "TC3" "retained=2"                                "$tc3_log"
assert_contains "TC3" "deleted=1"                                 "$tc3_log"
assert_contains "TC3" "Job succeeded"                             "$tc3_log"
rm -rf "$LAST_CASE_TMPDIR"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 4: Test Case 4 — combined policies (max-age + keep-latest)
# Expected: DELETE old-v1 old-v2 (max_age) + new-v1 (keep_latest)
# space_reclaimed=3145728 (3 × 1048576)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " SECTION 4: TC4 — combined policies"
echo "======================================================="

tc4_log=$(run_act_for_test_case \
    "TC4: combined policies" \
    "tc4" \
    "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/test_combined.csv")

assert_contains "TC4" "DELETE old-v1 max_age 1048576"        "$tc4_log"
assert_contains "TC4" "DELETE old-v2 max_age 1048576"        "$tc4_log"
assert_contains "TC4" "DELETE new-v1 keep_latest 1048576"    "$tc4_log"
assert_contains "TC4" "RETAIN new-v2"                         "$tc4_log"
assert_contains "TC4" "RETAIN new-v3"                         "$tc4_log"
assert_contains "TC4" "space_reclaimed=3145728"               "$tc4_log"
assert_contains "TC4" "total=5"                               "$tc4_log"
assert_contains "TC4" "retained=2"                            "$tc4_log"
assert_contains "TC4" "deleted=3"                             "$tc4_log"
assert_contains "TC4" "Job succeeded"                         "$tc4_log"
rm -rf "$LAST_CASE_TMPDIR"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 5: Test Case 5 — dry-run mode
# Expected: DRY-RUN MODE header, same plan as TC1
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " SECTION 5: TC5 — dry-run mode"
echo "======================================================="

tc5_log=$(run_act_for_test_case \
    "TC5: dry-run mode" \
    "tc5" \
    "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/test_dry_run.csv")

assert_contains "TC5" "DRY-RUN MODE"                          "$tc5_log"
assert_contains "TC5" "DELETE old-artifact max_age 1048576"   "$tc5_log"
assert_contains "TC5" "RETAIN new-artifact"                    "$tc5_log"
assert_contains "TC5" "space_reclaimed=1048576"                "$tc5_log"
assert_contains "TC5" "Job succeeded"                          "$tc5_log"
rm -rf "$LAST_CASE_TMPDIR"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Final summary
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " RESULTS"
echo "======================================================="
echo "  Passed: $PASS_COUNT"
echo "  Failed: $FAIL_COUNT"
echo ""
echo "  act-result.txt written to: $ACT_RESULT"

{
    echo ""
    echo "################################################################"
    echo "# HARNESS SUMMARY"
    echo "# Passed: $PASS_COUNT"
    echo "# Failed: $FAIL_COUNT"
    echo "################################################################"
} >> "$ACT_RESULT"

if [[ "$FAIL_COUNT" -gt 0 ]]; then
    echo "HARNESS FAILED: $FAIL_COUNT assertion(s) failed" >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "All assertions passed."
exit 0

1. Spin up Docker containers
2. Run lint job (apt-get update + shellcheck)
3. Run unit-tests job (apt-get update + bats + run 12 tests)
4. Run integration job (run specific TC_ID)


5. This will take 3-5 minutes per test case, so ~15-25 minutes total for all 5.

Let me start with just one test case to verify everything works before committing to the full run. I'm concerned about whether the TC_ID environment variable will be accessible in the integration job's run step, whether the checkout action will work properly in act, and what platform issues might come up with the Ubuntu image.

content.thinking:
There are two issues:
1. "File name too long" - the tmp dir path is being used as a file path? Let me check.
2. `LAST_CASE_TMPDIR: unbound variable` - this is because when `run_act_for_test_case` returns via `echo "$case_log"`, the `LAST_CASE_TMPDIR` variable is set inside the subshell but not exported to the parent.

Wait, the function `run_act_for_test_case` calls `export LAST_CASE_TMPDIR="$tmpdir"` - but since it's a function (not a subshell), the export should work... 

Oh, but I'm calling it in a command substitution with `tc1_log=$(run_act_for_test_case ...)`, which runs the function in a subshell, so the exported variable doesn't propagate back to the parent shell. As for the "File name too long" error from the grep command - that path looks suspiciously malformed, like the tmpdir itself is being passed as a filename somehow.

Looking more carefully at the function, I see the issue now: when I echo the log path at the end, the `$(...)` captures not just that path but all the output from the function combined together. So `tc1_log` ends up containing the entire act output plus the log file path concatenated, which is why grep is choking on it.

The cleanest fix is to redirect all the logging and assertion output to stderr while only echoing the log file path to stdout, so the command substitution captures just the path I need. I'm realizing the subshell issue means I can't rely on variable assignment, so I need to use a temp file as an intermediary—the function writes the log path to a file, then the harness reads it back after the function completes.

Actually, simpler approach: since calling the function directly (without command substitution) keeps it in the same shell, I can just use global variables that the function sets and the caller reads afterward. The function won't echo anything to stdout, so the caller can still print its own output without interference.

Now I'm hitting a different issue—the "File name too long" error appearing before the function even finishes. The path `/tmp/tmp.SLh1Iy0E2K/act_output.txt` is only 36 characters, so it's not actually the filename length that's the problem. This might be a Docker-specific issue where the container is interpreting the host path differently.

Looking at where `tee` writes the output, it runs on the host and successfully writes to the file—I can see the act output in the terminal. So the error must be coming from somewhere else entirely. The test assertion shows `[FAIL] TC1: expected 'Job succeeded' not found in output`, which suggests the real issue is that the expected string isn't in the captured output.

Then there's the unbound variable error for `LAST_CASE_TMPDIR`—that's because the `export` inside the command substitution doesn't propagate back to the parent shell. And looking at the "File name too long" error more carefully, it seems `assert_contains` is receiving the full act output content directly rather than a file path, which is why grep is choking on it. The real issue is that `grep` is receiving the entire captured output as a filename argument instead of a file path, which causes the "filename too long" error. The root problem is that command substitution captures everything the function outputs—the log messages, act output, and the final path—so `tc1_log` becomes a multi-line string rather than just the file path. Combined with `LAST_CASE_TMPDIR` not propagating out of the subshell, I need to redesign this to avoid command substitution altogether.

Refactoring with global variables...

content.text: The harness has a design flaw: calling the function via `$()` captures all stdout (including act output), making the returned log path unusable. Let me fix by using globals instead of command substitution:

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_act_tests.sh - Test harness that runs the GitHub Actions workflow via act
#
# For each test case, this harness:
#   1. Creates a temporary git repo with the project files and that case's fixture
#   2. Runs `act push --rm` against the workflow
#   3. Captures output to act-result.txt (appended, clearly delimited)
#   4. Asserts exact expected values appear in the output
#   5. Asserts every job shows "Job succeeded"
#
# Additionally verifies:
#   - Workflow YAML structure (triggers, jobs, steps)
#   - File references in the workflow are valid
#   - actionlint passes

set -euo pipefail

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Configuration
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
ACT_RESULT="${SCRIPT_DIR}/act-result.txt"
PLATFORM="ubuntu-latest=catthehacker/ubuntu:act-latest"
WORKFLOW_FILE=".github/workflows/artifact-cleanup-script.yml"

# Clear results file
: > "$ACT_RESULT"

PASS_COUNT=0
FAIL_COUNT=0

# Global variables set by run_act_for_test_case (avoids subshell capture issues)
CASE_LOG=""
CASE_TMPDIR=""

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
log()  { echo "[INFO]  $*"; }
pass() { echo "[PASS]  $*"; PASS_COUNT=$(( PASS_COUNT + 1 )); }
fail() { echo "[FAIL]  $*" >&2; FAIL_COUNT=$(( FAIL_COUNT + 1 )); }

# assert_contains LABEL EXPECTED FILE_PATH
# Checks that FILE_PATH contains the EXPECTED string; records pass/fail.
assert_contains() {
    local label="$1" expected="$2" file="$3"
    if grep -qF "$expected" "$file"; then
        pass "$label: found '$expected'"
    else
        fail "$label: expected '$expected' not found in output"
    fi
}

# run_act_for_test_case LABEL TC_ID FIXTURE_SRC
# Creates an isolated temp git repo, copies project files + the given fixture,
# runs act with TC_ID env var, appends to act-result.txt.
# Results written to global CASE_LOG (log file path) and CASE_TMPDIR.
run_act_for_test_case() {
    local label="$1"
    local tc_id="$2"
    local fixture_src="$3"

    local tmpdir
    tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
    CASE_TMPDIR="$tmpdir"

    log "Setting up temp repo for $label in $tmpdir"

    # --- Copy project files into temp repo ---
    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/artifact_cleanup.sh" "$tmpdir/"
    mkdir -p "$tmpdir/tests/fixtures"
    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/artifact_cleanup.bats" "$tmpdir/tests/"
    # Copy all fixture files so bats tests can find them
    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/"*.csv "$tmpdir/tests/fixtures/"
    mkdir -p "$tmpdir/.github/workflows"
    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE" "$tmpdir/$WORKFLOW_FILE"

    # --- Initialise git repo (actions/checkout@v4 needs a git history) ---
    git -C "$tmpdir" init -q
    git -C "$tmpdir" config user.email "test@example.com"
    git -C "$tmpdir" config user.name "Test"
    git -C "$tmpdir" add -A
    git -C "$tmpdir" commit -qm "test: $label"

    local case_log="$tmpdir/act_output.txt"
    CASE_LOG="$case_log"

    log "Running act for $label (TC_ID=$tc_id)..."
    set +e
    act push \
        --rm \
        --platform "$PLATFORM" \
        --env "TC_ID=$tc_id" \
        --env "REFDATE=9999999999" \
        --no-cache-server \
        --directory "$tmpdir" \
        2>&1 | tee "$case_log"
    local act_exit="${PIPESTATUS[0]}"
    set -e

    # --- Append to global act-result.txt with clear delimiter ---
    {
        echo ""
        echo "################################################################"
        echo "# TEST CASE: $label (TC_ID=$tc_id)"
        echo "# act exit code: $act_exit"
        echo "################################################################"
        cat "$case_log"
        echo "################################################################"
        echo "# END: $label"
        echo "################################################################"
    } >> "$ACT_RESULT"

    # --- Assert act exit code ---
    if [[ "$act_exit" -eq 0 ]]; then
        pass "$label: act exited 0"
    else
        fail "$label: act exited $act_exit (non-zero)"
    fi
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 0: Workflow structure and static checks
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " SECTION 0: Workflow Structure & Static Validation"
echo "======================================================="

{
    echo ""
    echo "################################################################"
    echo "# SECTION 0: Workflow Structure & Static Validation"
    echo "################################################################"
} >> "$ACT_RESULT"

# Check that workflow file exists
if [[ -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE" ]]; then
    pass "Workflow file exists at $WORKFLOW_FILE"
    echo "STRUCTURE: workflow file exists at $WORKFLOW_FILE" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
else
    fail "Workflow file missing: $WORKFLOW_FILE"
fi

# Verify workflow references correct script path
if grep -qF "artifact_cleanup.sh" "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE"; then
    pass "Workflow references artifact_cleanup.sh"
    echo "STRUCTURE: workflow references artifact_cleanup.sh" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
else
    fail "Workflow does not reference artifact_cleanup.sh"
fi

# Verify script file exists
if [[ -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/artifact_cleanup.sh" ]]; then
    pass "Script file artifact_cleanup.sh exists"
else
    fail "Script file artifact_cleanup.sh missing"
fi

# Verify fixture files referenced in workflow exist
for fixture in test_max_age test_keep_latest test_max_size test_combined test_dry_run; do
    fixture_path="$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/${fixture}.csv"
    if [[ -f "$fixture_path" ]]; then
        pass "Fixture exists: tests/fixtures/${fixture}.csv"
    else
        fail "Fixture missing: tests/fixtures/${fixture}.csv"
    fi
done

# Verify actionlint passes
log "Running actionlint..."
actionlint_output=$(actionlint "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE" 2>&1) && actionlint_exit=0 || actionlint_exit=$?
echo "ACTIONLINT EXIT: $actionlint_exit" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
echo "$actionlint_output" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
if [[ "$actionlint_exit" -eq 0 ]]; then
    pass "actionlint: workflow passes"
else
    fail "actionlint: workflow has errors: $actionlint_output"
fi

# Verify workflow has expected trigger events
for trigger in push pull_request workflow_dispatch schedule; do
    if grep -qF "$trigger" "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE"; then
        pass "Workflow has trigger: $trigger"
    else
        fail "Workflow missing trigger: $trigger"
    fi
done

# Verify expected job names exist in workflow
for job in lint unit-tests integration; do
    if grep -qF "$job" "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE"; then
        pass "Workflow has job: $job"
    else
        fail "Workflow missing job: $job"
    fi
done

# Static analysis on the main script
if shellcheck "$SCRIPT_DIR/artifact_cleanup.sh" 2>/dev/null; then
    pass "shellcheck: artifact_cleanup.sh passes"
    echo "SHELLCHECK: passed" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
else
    fail "shellcheck: artifact_cleanup.sh has warnings"
fi

if bash -n "$SCRIPT_DIR/artifact_cleanup.sh" 2>/dev/null; then
    pass "bash -n: artifact_cleanup.sh syntax valid"
else
    fail "bash -n: artifact_cleanup.sh syntax error"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 1: Test Case 1 — max-age policy
# Expected: DELETE old-artifact, RETAIN new-artifact; space_reclaimed=1048576
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------

echo "======================================================="
echo " SECTION 1: TC1 — max-age policy"

assert_contains "TC1" "DELETE old-artifact max_age 1048576" "$CASE_LOG"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------

echo "======================================================="

run_act_for_test_case \

assert_contains "TC2" "DELETE artifact-v1 keep_latest 524288" "$CASE_LOG"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 3: Test Case 3 — max-total-size policy
# Expected: DELETE artifact-a (oldest 3 MB), RETAIN artifact-b artifact-c
# space_reclaimed=3145728
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " SECTION 3: TC3 — max-total-size policy"
echo "======================================================="

run_act_for_test_case \
    "TC3: max-total-size policy" \
    "tc3" \
    "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/test_max_size.csv"

assert_contains "TC3" "DELETE artifact-a max_total_size 3145728" "$CASE_LOG"

assert_contains "TC3" "RETAIN artifact-c"                         "$CASE_LOG"

assert_contains "TC4" "RETAIN new-v2"                         "$CASE_LOG"

assert_contains "TC4" "Job succeeded"                         "$CASE_LOG"

} >> "$ACT_RESULT"

    echo "HARNESS FAILED: $FAIL_COUNT assertion(s) failed" >&2

fi

echo "All assertions passed."
exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_act_tests.sh - Test harness that runs the GitHub Actions workflow via act
#
# For each test case, this harness:
#   1. Creates a temporary git repo with the project files and that case's fixture
#   2. Runs `act push --rm` against the workflow
#   3. Captures output to act-result.txt (appended, clearly delimited)
#   4. Asserts exact expected values appear in the output
#   5. Asserts every job shows "Job succeeded"
#
# Additionally verifies:
#   - Workflow YAML structure (triggers, jobs, steps)
#   - File references in the workflow are valid
#   - actionlint passes

set -euo pipefail

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Configuration
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
ACT_RESULT="${SCRIPT_DIR}/act-result.txt"
PLATFORM="ubuntu-latest=catthehacker/ubuntu:act-latest"
WORKFLOW_FILE=".github/workflows/artifact-cleanup-script.yml"

# Clear results file
: > "$ACT_RESULT"

PASS_COUNT=0
FAIL_COUNT=0

# Global variables set by run_act_for_test_case (avoids subshell capture issues)
CASE_LOG=""
CASE_TMPDIR=""

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
log()  { echo "[INFO]  $*"; }
pass() { echo "[PASS]  $*"; PASS_COUNT=$(( PASS_COUNT + 1 )); }
fail() { echo "[FAIL]  $*" >&2; FAIL_COUNT=$(( FAIL_COUNT + 1 )); }

# assert_contains LABEL EXPECTED FILE_PATH
# Checks that FILE_PATH contains the EXPECTED string; records pass/fail.
assert_contains() {
    local label="$1" expected="$2" file="$3"
    if grep -qF "$expected" "$file"; then
        pass "$label: found '$expected'"
    else
        fail "$label: expected '$expected' not found in output"
    fi
}

# run_act_for_test_case LABEL TC_ID FIXTURE_SRC
# Creates an isolated temp git repo, copies project files + the given fixture,
# runs act with TC_ID env var, appends to act-result.txt.
# Results written to global CASE_LOG (log file path) and CASE_TMPDIR.
run_act_for_test_case() {
    local label="$1"
    local tc_id="$2"
    local fixture_src="$3"

    local tmpdir
    tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
    CASE_TMPDIR="$tmpdir"

    log "Setting up temp repo for $label in $tmpdir"

    # --- Copy project files into temp repo ---
    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/artifact_cleanup.sh" "$tmpdir/"
    mkdir -p "$tmpdir/tests/fixtures"
    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/artifact_cleanup.bats" "$tmpdir/tests/"
    # Copy all fixture files so bats tests can find them
    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/"*.csv "$tmpdir/tests/fixtures/"
    mkdir -p "$tmpdir/.github/workflows"
    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE" "$tmpdir/$WORKFLOW_FILE"

    # --- Initialise git repo (actions/checkout@v4 needs a git history) ---
    git -C "$tmpdir" init -q
    git -C "$tmpdir" config user.email "test@example.com"
    git -C "$tmpdir" config user.name "Test"
    git -C "$tmpdir" add -A
    git -C "$tmpdir" commit -qm "test: $label"

    local case_log="$tmpdir/act_output.txt"
    CASE_LOG="$case_log"

    log "Running act for $label (TC_ID=$tc_id)..."
    set +e
    act push \
        --rm \
        --platform "$PLATFORM" \
        --env "TC_ID=$tc_id" \
        --env "REFDATE=9999999999" \
        --no-cache-server \
        --directory "$tmpdir" \
        2>&1 | tee "$case_log"
    local act_exit="${PIPESTATUS[0]}"
    set -e

    # --- Append to global act-result.txt with clear delimiter ---
    {
        echo ""
        echo "################################################################"
        echo "# TEST CASE: $label (TC_ID=$tc_id)"
        echo "# act exit code: $act_exit"
        echo "################################################################"
        cat "$case_log"
        echo "################################################################"
        echo "# END: $label"
        echo "################################################################"
    } >> "$ACT_RESULT"

    # --- Assert act exit code ---
    if [[ "$act_exit" -eq 0 ]]; then
        pass "$label: act exited 0"
    else
        fail "$label: act exited $act_exit (non-zero)"
    fi
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 0: Workflow structure and static checks
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " SECTION 0: Workflow Structure & Static Validation"
echo "======================================================="

{
    echo ""
    echo "################################################################"
    echo "# SECTION 0: Workflow Structure & Static Validation"
    echo "################################################################"
} >> "$ACT_RESULT"

# Check that workflow file exists
if [[ -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE" ]]; then
    pass "Workflow file exists at $WORKFLOW_FILE"
    echo "STRUCTURE: workflow file exists at $WORKFLOW_FILE" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
else
    fail "Workflow file missing: $WORKFLOW_FILE"
fi

# Verify workflow references correct script path
if grep -qF "artifact_cleanup.sh" "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE"; then
    pass "Workflow references artifact_cleanup.sh"
    echo "STRUCTURE: workflow references artifact_cleanup.sh" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
else
    fail "Workflow does not reference artifact_cleanup.sh"
fi

# Verify script file exists
if [[ -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/artifact_cleanup.sh" ]]; then
    pass "Script file artifact_cleanup.sh exists"
else
    fail "Script file artifact_cleanup.sh missing"
fi

# Verify fixture files referenced in workflow exist
for fixture in test_max_age test_keep_latest test_max_size test_combined test_dry_run; do
    fixture_path="$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/${fixture}.csv"
    if [[ -f "$fixture_path" ]]; then
        pass "Fixture exists: tests/fixtures/${fixture}.csv"
    else
        fail "Fixture missing: tests/fixtures/${fixture}.csv"
    fi
done

# Verify actionlint passes
log "Running actionlint..."
actionlint_output=$(actionlint "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE" 2>&1) && actionlint_exit=0 || actionlint_exit=$?
echo "ACTIONLINT EXIT: $actionlint_exit" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
echo "$actionlint_output" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
if [[ "$actionlint_exit" -eq 0 ]]; then
    pass "actionlint: workflow passes"
else
    fail "actionlint: workflow has errors: $actionlint_output"
fi

# Verify workflow has expected trigger events
for trigger in push pull_request workflow_dispatch schedule; do
    if grep -qF "$trigger" "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE"; then
        pass "Workflow has trigger: $trigger"
    else
        fail "Workflow missing trigger: $trigger"
    fi
done

# Verify expected job names exist in workflow
for job in lint unit-tests integration; do
    if grep -qF "$job" "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE"; then
        pass "Workflow has job: $job"
    else
        fail "Workflow missing job: $job"
    fi
done

# Static analysis on the main script
if shellcheck "$SCRIPT_DIR/artifact_cleanup.sh" 2>/dev/null; then
    pass "shellcheck: artifact_cleanup.sh passes"
    echo "SHELLCHECK: passed" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
else
    fail "shellcheck: artifact_cleanup.sh has warnings"
fi

if bash -n "$SCRIPT_DIR/artifact_cleanup.sh" 2>/dev/null; then
    pass "bash -n: artifact_cleanup.sh syntax valid"
else
    fail "bash -n: artifact_cleanup.sh syntax error"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 1: Test Case 1 — max-age policy
# Expected: DELETE old-artifact, RETAIN new-artifact; space_reclaimed=1048576
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " SECTION 1: TC1 — max-age policy"
echo "======================================================="

run_act_for_test_case \
    "TC1: max-age policy" \
    "tc1" \
    "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/test_max_age.csv"

assert_contains "TC1" "DELETE old-artifact max_age 1048576" "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC1" "RETAIN new-artifact"                  "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC1" "space_reclaimed=1048576"              "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC1" "total=2"                              "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC1" "retained=1"                           "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC1" "deleted=1"                            "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC1" "Job succeeded"                        "$CASE_LOG"
rm -rf "$CASE_TMPDIR"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 2: Test Case 2 — keep-latest-N policy
# Expected: DELETE artifact-v1 (oldest), RETAIN artifact-v2 artifact-v3
# space_reclaimed=524288
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " SECTION 2: TC2 — keep-latest-N policy"
echo "======================================================="

run_act_for_test_case \
    "TC2: keep-latest-N policy" \
    "tc2" \
    "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/test_keep_latest.csv"

assert_contains "TC2" "DELETE artifact-v1 keep_latest 524288" "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC2" "RETAIN artifact-v2"                     "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC2" "RETAIN artifact-v3"                     "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC2" "space_reclaimed=524288"                 "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC2" "total=3"                                "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC2" "retained=2"                             "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC2" "deleted=1"                              "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC2" "Job succeeded"                          "$CASE_LOG"
rm -rf "$CASE_TMPDIR"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 3: Test Case 3 — max-total-size policy
# Expected: DELETE artifact-a (oldest 3 MB), RETAIN artifact-b artifact-c
# space_reclaimed=3145728
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " SECTION 3: TC3 — max-total-size policy"
echo "======================================================="

run_act_for_test_case \
    "TC3: max-total-size policy" \
    "tc3" \
    "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/test_max_size.csv"

assert_contains "TC3" "DELETE artifact-a max_total_size 3145728" "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC3" "RETAIN artifact-b"                         "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC3" "RETAIN artifact-c"                         "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC3" "space_reclaimed=3145728"                   "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC3" "total=3"                                   "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC3" "retained=2"                                "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC3" "deleted=1"                                 "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC3" "Job succeeded"                             "$CASE_LOG"
rm -rf "$CASE_TMPDIR"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 4: Test Case 4 — combined policies (max-age + keep-latest)
# Expected: DELETE old-v1 old-v2 (max_age) + new-v1 (keep_latest)
# space_reclaimed=3145728 (3 × 1048576)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " SECTION 4: TC4 — combined policies"
echo "======================================================="

run_act_for_test_case \
    "TC4: combined policies" \
    "tc4" \

assert_contains "TC4" "DELETE old-v1 max_age 1048576"        "$CASE_LOG"

    fi
}

# run_act_for_test_case LABEL TC_ID FIXTURE_SRC EXTRA_ENV...
# Creates an isolated temp git repo, copies project files + the given fixture
# as tests/fixtures/<name>.csv, runs act, appends to act-result.txt, and
# returns the path to the per-case log file.
run_act_for_test_case() {
    local label="$1"
    local tc_id="$2"
    local fixture_src="$3"
    shift 3
    local extra_env=("$@")  # array of "--env KEY=VAL" pairs

    local tmpdir
    tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
    # Ensure cleanup even on failure
    # shellcheck disable=SC2064
    trap "rm -rf '$tmpdir'" EXIT INT TERM

    log "Setting up temp repo for $label in $tmpdir"

    # --- Copy project files into temp repo ---
    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/artifact_cleanup.sh" "$tmpdir/"
    mkdir -p "$tmpdir/tests/fixtures"
    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/artifact_cleanup.bats" "$tmpdir/tests/"
    # Copy all fixture files so bats tests can find them
    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/"*.csv "$tmpdir/tests/fixtures/"
    # Copy the test-case-specific fixture as the canonical one too
    cp "$fixture_src" "$tmpdir/tests/fixtures/current_fixture.csv"
    mkdir -p "$tmpdir/.github/workflows"
    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE" "$tmpdir/$WORKFLOW_FILE"

    # --- Initialise git repo (actions/checkout@v4 needs a git history) ---
    git -C "$tmpdir" init -q
    git -C "$tmpdir" config user.email "test@example.com"
    git -C "$tmpdir" config user.name "Test"
    git -C "$tmpdir" add -A
    git -C "$tmpdir" commit -qm "test: $label"

    # --- Build act command ---
    local act_cmd=(
        act push
        --rm
        --platform "$PLATFORM"
        --env "TC_ID=$tc_id"
        --env "REFDATE=9999999999"
        --no-cache-server
        --directory "$tmpdir"
    )
    for env_pair in "${extra_env[@]+"${extra_env[@]}"}"; do
        act_cmd+=(--env "$env_pair")
    done

    local case_log="$tmpdir/act_output.txt"

    log "Running act for $label (TC_ID=$tc_id)..."
    set +e
    "${act_cmd[@]}" 2>&1 | tee "$case_log"
    local act_exit=$?
    set -e

    # --- Append to global act-result.txt with clear delimiter ---
    {
        echo ""
        echo "################################################################"
        echo "# TEST CASE: $label (TC_ID=$tc_id)"
        echo "# act exit code: $act_exit"
        echo "################################################################"
        cat "$case_log"
        echo "################################################################"
        echo "# END: $label"
        echo "################################################################"
    } >> "$ACT_RESULT"

    # --- Assert act exit code ---
    if [[ "$act_exit" -eq 0 ]]; then
        pass "$label: act exited 0"
    else
        fail "$label: act exited $act_exit (non-zero)"
    fi

    echo "$case_log"  # return log path for caller to assert against
    # Reset the EXIT trap before returning so the caller's temp dir isn't removed
    trap - EXIT INT TERM
    # The tmpdir will be cleaned up naturally when the subshell exits;
    # we keep it alive until the caller is done asserting.
    export LAST_CASE_TMPDIR="$tmpdir"
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 0: Workflow structure and static checks
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " SECTION 0: Workflow Structure & Static Validation"
echo "======================================================="

{
    echo ""
    echo "################################################################"
    echo "# SECTION 0: Workflow Structure & Static Validation"
    echo "################################################################"
} >> "$ACT_RESULT"

# Check that workflow file exists
if [[ -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE" ]]; then
    pass "Workflow file exists at $WORKFLOW_FILE"
    echo "STRUCTURE: workflow file exists at $WORKFLOW_FILE" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
else
    fail "Workflow file missing: $WORKFLOW_FILE"
fi

# Verify workflow references correct script path
if grep -qF "artifact_cleanup.sh" "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE"; then
    pass "Workflow references artifact_cleanup.sh"
    echo "STRUCTURE: workflow references artifact_cleanup.sh" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
else
    fail "Workflow does not reference artifact_cleanup.sh"
fi

# Verify script file exists
if [[ -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/artifact_cleanup.sh" ]]; then
    pass "Script file artifact_cleanup.sh exists"
else
    fail "Script file artifact_cleanup.sh missing"
fi

# Verify fixture files referenced in workflow exist
for fixture in test_max_age test_keep_latest test_max_size test_combined test_dry_run; do
    fixture_path="$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/${fixture}.csv"
    if [[ -f "$fixture_path" ]]; then
        pass "Fixture exists: tests/fixtures/${fixture}.csv"
    else
        fail "Fixture missing: tests/fixtures/${fixture}.csv"
    fi
done

# Verify actionlint passes
log "Running actionlint..."
actionlint_output=$(actionlint "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE" 2>&1) && actionlint_exit=0 || actionlint_exit=$?
echo "ACTIONLINT EXIT: $actionlint_exit" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
echo "$actionlint_output" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
if [[ "$actionlint_exit" -eq 0 ]]; then
    pass "actionlint: workflow passes"
else
    fail "actionlint: workflow has errors: $actionlint_output"
fi

# Verify workflow has expected trigger events
for trigger in push pull_request workflow_dispatch schedule; do
    if grep -qF "$trigger" "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE"; then
        pass "Workflow has trigger: $trigger"
    else
        fail "Workflow missing trigger: $trigger"
    fi
done

# Verify expected job names exist in workflow
for job in lint unit-tests integration; do
    if grep -qF "$job" "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE"; then
        pass "Workflow has job: $job"
    else
        fail "Workflow missing job: $job"
    fi
done

# Static analysis on the main script
if shellcheck "$SCRIPT_DIR/artifact_cleanup.sh" 2>/dev/null; then
    pass "shellcheck: artifact_cleanup.sh passes"
    echo "SHELLCHECK: passed" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
else
    fail "shellcheck: artifact_cleanup.sh has warnings"
fi

if bash -n "$SCRIPT_DIR/artifact_cleanup.sh" 2>/dev/null; then
    pass "bash -n: artifact_cleanup.sh syntax valid"
else
    fail "bash -n: artifact_cleanup.sh syntax error"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 1: Test Case 1 — max-age policy
# Expected: DELETE old-artifact, RETAIN new-artifact; space_reclaimed=1048576
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " SECTION 1: TC1 — max-age policy"
echo "======================================================="

tc1_log=$(run_act_for_test_case \
    "TC1: max-age policy" \
    "tc1" \
    "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/test_max_age.csv")

assert_contains "TC1" "DELETE old-artifact max_age 1048576" "$tc1_log"
assert_contains "TC1" "RETAIN new-artifact"                  "$tc1_log"
assert_contains "TC1" "space_reclaimed=1048576"              "$tc1_log"
assert_contains "TC1" "total=2"                              "$tc1_log"
assert_contains "TC1" "retained=1"                           "$tc1_log"
assert_contains "TC1" "deleted=1"                            "$tc1_log"
assert_contains "TC1" "Job succeeded"                        "$tc1_log"
rm -rf "$LAST_CASE_TMPDIR"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------

# Expected: DELETE artifact-v1 (oldest), RETAIN artifact-v2 artifact-v3
# space_reclaimed=524288
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------

echo "======================================================="

assert_contains "TC2" "DELETE artifact-v1 keep_latest 524288" "$tc2_log"

assert_contains "TC2" "retained=2"                             "$tc2_log"
assert_contains "TC2" "deleted=1"                              "$tc2_log"
assert_contains "TC2" "Job succeeded"                          "$tc2_log"
rm -rf "$LAST_CASE_TMPDIR"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 3: Test Case 3 — max-total-size policy
# Expected: DELETE artifact-a (oldest 3 MB), RETAIN artifact-b artifact-c
# space_reclaimed=3145728
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " SECTION 3: TC3 — max-total-size policy"
echo "======================================================="

tc3_log=$(run_act_for_test_case \
    "TC3: max-total-size policy" \
    "tc3" \
    "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/test_max_size.csv")

assert_contains "TC3" "DELETE artifact-a max_total_size 3145728" "$tc3_log"
assert_contains "TC3" "RETAIN artifact-b"                         "$tc3_log"
assert_contains "TC3" "RETAIN artifact-c"                         "$tc3_log"
assert_contains "TC3" "space_reclaimed=3145728"                   "$tc3_log"
assert_contains "TC3" "total=3"                                   "$tc3_log"
assert_contains "TC3" "retained=2"                                "$tc3_log"
assert_contains "TC3" "deleted=1"                                 "$tc3_log"
assert_contains "TC3" "Job succeeded"                             "$tc3_log"
rm -rf "$LAST_CASE_TMPDIR"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 4: Test Case 4 — combined policies (max-age + keep-latest)
# Expected: DELETE old-v1 old-v2 (max_age) + new-v1 (keep_latest)
# space_reclaimed=3145728 (3 × 1048576)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " SECTION 4: TC4 — combined policies"
echo "======================================================="

tc4_log=$(run_act_for_test_case \
    "TC4: combined policies" \
    "tc4" \
    "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/test_combined.csv")

assert_contains "TC4" "DELETE old-v1 max_age 1048576"        "$tc4_log"
assert_contains "TC4" "DELETE old-v2 max_age 1048576"        "$tc4_log"
assert_contains "TC4" "DELETE new-v1 keep_latest 1048576"    "$tc4_log"
assert_contains "TC4" "RETAIN new-v2"                         "$tc4_log"
assert_contains "TC4" "RETAIN new-v3"                         "$tc4_log"
assert_contains "TC4" "space_reclaimed=3145728"               "$tc4_log"
assert_contains "TC4" "total=5"                               "$tc4_log"
assert_contains "TC4" "retained=2"                            "$tc4_log"
assert_contains "TC4" "deleted=3"                             "$tc4_log"
assert_contains "TC4" "Job succeeded"                         "$tc4_log"
rm -rf "$LAST_CASE_TMPDIR"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 5: Test Case 5 — dry-run mode
# Expected: DRY-RUN MODE header, same plan as TC1
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " SECTION 5: TC5 — dry-run mode"
echo "======================================================="

tc5_log=$(run_act_for_test_case \
    "TC5: dry-run mode" \
    "tc5" \
    "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/test_dry_run.csv")

assert_contains "TC5" "DRY-RUN MODE"                          "$tc5_log"

assert_contains "TC5" "space_reclaimed=1048576"                "$tc5_log"
assert_contains "TC5" "Job succeeded"                          "$tc5_log"
rm -rf "$LAST_CASE_TMPDIR"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Final summary
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " RESULTS"
echo "======================================================="
echo "  Passed: $PASS_COUNT"
echo "  Failed: $FAIL_COUNT"
echo ""
echo "  act-result.txt written to: $ACT_RESULT"

{
    echo ""
    echo "################################################################"
    echo "# HARNESS SUMMARY"
    echo "# Passed: $PASS_COUNT"
    echo "# Failed: $FAIL_COUNT"
    echo "################################################################"
} >> "$ACT_RESULT"

if [[ "$FAIL_COUNT" -gt 0 ]]; then
    echo "HARNESS FAILED: $FAIL_COUNT assertion(s) failed" >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "All assertions passed."
exit 0

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_act_tests.sh - Test harness that runs the GitHub Actions workflow via act
#
# For each test case, this harness:
#   1. Creates a temporary git repo with the project files and that case's fixture
#   2. Runs `act push --rm` against the workflow
#   3. Captures output to act-result.txt (appended, clearly delimited)
#   4. Asserts exact expected values appear in the output
#   5. Asserts every job shows "Job succeeded"
#
# Additionally verifies:
#   - Workflow YAML structure (triggers, jobs, steps)
#   - File references in the workflow are valid
#   - actionlint passes

set -euo pipefail

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Configuration
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
ACT_RESULT="${SCRIPT_DIR}/act-result.txt"
PLATFORM="ubuntu-latest=catthehacker/ubuntu:act-latest"
WORKFLOW_FILE=".github/workflows/artifact-cleanup-script.yml"

# Clear results file
: > "$ACT_RESULT"

PASS_COUNT=0
FAIL_COUNT=0

# Global variables set by run_act_for_test_case (avoids subshell capture issues)
CASE_LOG=""
CASE_TMPDIR=""

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
log()  { echo "[INFO]  $*"; }
pass() { echo "[PASS]  $*"; PASS_COUNT=$(( PASS_COUNT + 1 )); }
fail() { echo "[FAIL]  $*" >&2; FAIL_COUNT=$(( FAIL_COUNT + 1 )); }

# assert_contains LABEL EXPECTED FILE_PATH
# Checks that FILE_PATH contains the EXPECTED string; records pass/fail.
assert_contains() {
    local label="$1" expected="$2" file="$3"
    if grep -qF "$expected" "$file"; then
        pass "$label: found '$expected'"
    else
        fail "$label: expected '$expected' not found in output"
    fi
}

# run_act_for_test_case LABEL TC_ID FIXTURE_SRC
# Creates an isolated temp git repo, copies project files + the given fixture,
# runs act with TC_ID env var, appends to act-result.txt.
# Results written to global CASE_LOG (log file path) and CASE_TMPDIR.
run_act_for_test_case() {
    local label="$1"
    local tc_id="$2"
    local fixture_src="$3"

    local tmpdir
    tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
    CASE_TMPDIR="$tmpdir"

    log "Setting up temp repo for $label in $tmpdir"

    # --- Copy project files into temp repo ---
    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/artifact_cleanup.sh" "$tmpdir/"
    mkdir -p "$tmpdir/tests/fixtures"
    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/artifact_cleanup.bats" "$tmpdir/tests/"
    # Copy all fixture files so bats tests can find them
    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/"*.csv "$tmpdir/tests/fixtures/"
    mkdir -p "$tmpdir/.github/workflows"
    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE" "$tmpdir/$WORKFLOW_FILE"

    # --- Initialise git repo (actions/checkout@v4 needs a git history) ---
    git -C "$tmpdir" init -q
    git -C "$tmpdir" config user.email "test@example.com"
    git -C "$tmpdir" config user.name "Test"
    git -C "$tmpdir" add -A
    git -C "$tmpdir" commit -qm "test: $label"

    local case_log="$tmpdir/act_output.txt"
    CASE_LOG="$case_log"

    log "Running act for $label (TC_ID=$tc_id)..."
    set +e
    act push \
        --rm \
        --platform "$PLATFORM" \
        --env "TC_ID=$tc_id" \
        --env "REFDATE=9999999999" \
        --no-cache-server \
        --directory "$tmpdir" \
        2>&1 | tee "$case_log"
    local act_exit="${PIPESTATUS[0]}"
    set -e

    # --- Append to global act-result.txt with clear delimiter ---
    {
        echo ""
        echo "################################################################"
        echo "# TEST CASE: $label (TC_ID=$tc_id)"
        echo "# act exit code: $act_exit"
        echo "################################################################"
        cat "$case_log"
        echo "################################################################"
        echo "# END: $label"
        echo "################################################################"
    } >> "$ACT_RESULT"

    # --- Assert act exit code ---
    if [[ "$act_exit" -eq 0 ]]; then
        pass "$label: act exited 0"
    else
        fail "$label: act exited $act_exit (non-zero)"
    fi
}

tool_use_result.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_act_tests.sh - Test harness that runs the GitHub Actions workflow via act
#
# For each test case, this harness:
#   1. Creates a temporary git repo with the project files and that case's fixture
#   2. Runs `act push --rm` against the workflow
#   3. Captures output to act-result.txt (appended, clearly delimited)
#   4. Asserts exact expected values appear in the output
#   5. Asserts every job shows "Job succeeded"
#
# Additionally verifies:
#   - Workflow YAML structure (triggers, jobs, steps)
#   - File references in the workflow are valid
#   - actionlint passes

set -euo pipefail

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Configuration
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
ACT_RESULT="${SCRIPT_DIR}/act-result.txt"
PLATFORM="ubuntu-latest=catthehacker/ubuntu:act-latest"
WORKFLOW_FILE=".github/workflows/artifact-cleanup-script.yml"

# Clear results file
: > "$ACT_RESULT"

PASS_COUNT=0
FAIL_COUNT=0

# Global variables set by run_act_for_test_case (avoids subshell capture issues)
CASE_LOG=""
CASE_TMPDIR=""

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
log()  { echo "[INFO]  $*"; }
pass() { echo "[PASS]  $*"; PASS_COUNT=$(( PASS_COUNT + 1 )); }
fail() { echo "[FAIL]  $*" >&2; FAIL_COUNT=$(( FAIL_COUNT + 1 )); }

# assert_contains LABEL EXPECTED FILE_PATH
# Checks that FILE_PATH contains the EXPECTED string; records pass/fail.
assert_contains() {
    local label="$1" expected="$2" file="$3"
    if grep -qF "$expected" "$file"; then
        pass "$label: found '$expected'"
    else
        fail "$label: expected '$expected' not found in output"
    fi
}

# run_act_for_test_case LABEL TC_ID FIXTURE_SRC
# Creates an isolated temp git repo, copies project files + the given fixture,
# runs act with TC_ID env var, appends to act-result.txt.
# Results written to global CASE_LOG (log file path) and CASE_TMPDIR.
run_act_for_test_case() {
    local label="$1"
    local tc_id="$2"
    # fixture_src ($3) — kept for documentation/future use; fixtures already in repo
    local tmpdir
    tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
    CASE_TMPDIR="$tmpdir"

    log "Setting up temp repo for $label in $tmpdir"

    # --- Copy project files into temp repo ---
    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/artifact_cleanup.sh" "$tmpdir/"
    mkdir -p "$tmpdir/tests/fixtures"
    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/artifact_cleanup.bats" "$tmpdir/tests/"
    # Copy all fixture files so bats tests can find them
    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/"*.csv "$tmpdir/tests/fixtures/"
    mkdir -p "$tmpdir/.github/workflows"
    cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE" "$tmpdir/$WORKFLOW_FILE"

    # --- Initialise git repo (actions/checkout@v4 needs a git history) ---
    git -C "$tmpdir" init -q
    git -C "$tmpdir" config user.email "test@example.com"
    git -C "$tmpdir" config user.name "Test"
    git -C "$tmpdir" add -A
    git -C "$tmpdir" commit -qm "test: $label"

    local case_log="$tmpdir/act_output.txt"
    CASE_LOG="$case_log"

    log "Running act for $label (TC_ID=$tc_id)..."
    set +e
    act push \
        --rm \
        --platform "$PLATFORM" \
        --env "TC_ID=$tc_id" \
        --env "REFDATE=9999999999" \
        --no-cache-server \
        --directory "$tmpdir" \
        2>&1 | tee "$case_log"
    local act_exit="${PIPESTATUS[0]}"
    set -e

    # --- Append to global act-result.txt with clear delimiter ---
    {
        echo ""
        echo "################################################################"
        echo "# TEST CASE: $label (TC_ID=$tc_id)"
        echo "# act exit code: $act_exit"
        echo "################################################################"
        cat "$case_log"
        echo "################################################################"
        echo "# END: $label"
        echo "################################################################"
    } >> "$ACT_RESULT"

    # --- Assert act exit code ---
    if [[ "$act_exit" -eq 0 ]]; then
        pass "$label: act exited 0"
    else
        fail "$label: act exited $act_exit (non-zero)"
    fi
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 0: Workflow structure and static checks
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " SECTION 0: Workflow Structure & Static Validation"
echo "======================================================="

{
    echo ""
    echo "################################################################"
    echo "# SECTION 0: Workflow Structure & Static Validation"
    echo "################################################################"
} >> "$ACT_RESULT"

# Check that workflow file exists
if [[ -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE" ]]; then
    pass "Workflow file exists at $WORKFLOW_FILE"
    echo "STRUCTURE: workflow file exists at $WORKFLOW_FILE" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
else
    fail "Workflow file missing: $WORKFLOW_FILE"
fi

# Verify workflow references correct script path
if grep -qF "artifact_cleanup.sh" "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE"; then
    pass "Workflow references artifact_cleanup.sh"
    echo "STRUCTURE: workflow references artifact_cleanup.sh" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
else
    fail "Workflow does not reference artifact_cleanup.sh"
fi

# Verify script file exists
if [[ -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/artifact_cleanup.sh" ]]; then
    pass "Script file artifact_cleanup.sh exists"
else
    fail "Script file artifact_cleanup.sh missing"
fi

# Verify fixture files referenced in workflow exist
for fixture in test_max_age test_keep_latest test_max_size test_combined test_dry_run; do
    fixture_path="$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/${fixture}.csv"
    if [[ -f "$fixture_path" ]]; then
        pass "Fixture exists: tests/fixtures/${fixture}.csv"
    else
        fail "Fixture missing: tests/fixtures/${fixture}.csv"
    fi
done

# Verify actionlint passes
log "Running actionlint..."
actionlint_output=$(actionlint "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE" 2>&1) && actionlint_exit=0 || actionlint_exit=$?
echo "ACTIONLINT EXIT: $actionlint_exit" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
echo "$actionlint_output" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
if [[ "$actionlint_exit" -eq 0 ]]; then
    pass "actionlint: workflow passes"
else
    fail "actionlint: workflow has errors: $actionlint_output"
fi

# Verify workflow has expected trigger events
for trigger in push pull_request workflow_dispatch schedule; do
    if grep -qF "$trigger" "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE"; then
        pass "Workflow has trigger: $trigger"
    else
        fail "Workflow missing trigger: $trigger"
    fi
done

# Verify expected job names exist in workflow
for job in lint unit-tests integration; do
    if grep -qF "$job" "$SCRIPT_DIR/$WORKFLOW_FILE"; then
        pass "Workflow has job: $job"
    else
        fail "Workflow missing job: $job"
    fi
done

# Static analysis on the main script
if shellcheck "$SCRIPT_DIR/artifact_cleanup.sh" 2>/dev/null; then
    pass "shellcheck: artifact_cleanup.sh passes"
    echo "SHELLCHECK: passed" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
else
    fail "shellcheck: artifact_cleanup.sh has warnings"
fi

if bash -n "$SCRIPT_DIR/artifact_cleanup.sh" 2>/dev/null; then
    pass "bash -n: artifact_cleanup.sh syntax valid"
else
    fail "bash -n: artifact_cleanup.sh syntax error"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 1: Test Case 1 — max-age policy
# Expected: DELETE old-artifact, RETAIN new-artifact; space_reclaimed=1048576
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " SECTION 1: TC1 — max-age policy"
echo "======================================================="

run_act_for_test_case \
    "TC1: max-age policy" \
    "tc1" \
    "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/test_max_age.csv"

assert_contains "TC1" "DELETE old-artifact max_age 1048576" "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC1" "RETAIN new-artifact"                  "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC1" "space_reclaimed=1048576"              "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC1" "total=2"                              "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC1" "retained=1"                           "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC1" "deleted=1"                            "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC1" "Job succeeded"                        "$CASE_LOG"
rm -rf "$CASE_TMPDIR"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 2: Test Case 2 — keep-latest-N policy
# Expected: DELETE artifact-v1 (oldest), RETAIN artifact-v2 artifact-v3
# space_reclaimed=524288
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " SECTION 2: TC2 — keep-latest-N policy"
echo "======================================================="

run_act_for_test_case \
    "TC2: keep-latest-N policy" \
    "tc2" \
    "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/test_keep_latest.csv"

assert_contains "TC2" "DELETE artifact-v1 keep_latest 524288" "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC2" "RETAIN artifact-v2"                     "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC2" "RETAIN artifact-v3"                     "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC2" "space_reclaimed=524288"                 "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC2" "total=3"                                "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC2" "retained=2"                             "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC2" "deleted=1"                              "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC2" "Job succeeded"                          "$CASE_LOG"
rm -rf "$CASE_TMPDIR"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 3: Test Case 3 — max-total-size policy
# Expected: DELETE artifact-a (oldest 3 MB), RETAIN artifact-b artifact-c
# space_reclaimed=3145728
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " SECTION 3: TC3 — max-total-size policy"
echo "======================================================="

run_act_for_test_case \
    "TC3: max-total-size policy" \
    "tc3" \
    "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/test_max_size.csv"

assert_contains "TC3" "DELETE artifact-a max_total_size 3145728" "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC3" "RETAIN artifact-b"                         "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC3" "RETAIN artifact-c"                         "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC3" "space_reclaimed=3145728"                   "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC3" "total=3"                                   "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC3" "retained=2"                                "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC3" "deleted=1"                                 "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC3" "Job succeeded"                             "$CASE_LOG"
rm -rf "$CASE_TMPDIR"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 4: Test Case 4 — combined policies (max-age + keep-latest)
# Expected: DELETE old-v1 old-v2 (max_age) + new-v1 (keep_latest)
# space_reclaimed=3145728 (3 × 1048576)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " SECTION 4: TC4 — combined policies"
echo "======================================================="

run_act_for_test_case \
    "TC4: combined policies" \
    "tc4" \
    "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/test_combined.csv"

assert_contains "TC4" "DELETE old-v1 max_age 1048576"        "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC4" "DELETE old-v2 max_age 1048576"        "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC4" "DELETE new-v1 keep_latest 1048576"    "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC4" "RETAIN new-v2"                         "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC4" "RETAIN new-v3"                         "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC4" "space_reclaimed=3145728"               "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC4" "total=5"                               "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC4" "retained=2"                            "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC4" "deleted=3"                             "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC4" "Job succeeded"                         "$CASE_LOG"
rm -rf "$CASE_TMPDIR"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# SECTION 5: Test Case 5 — dry-run mode
# Expected: DRY-RUN MODE header, same plan as TC1
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " SECTION 5: TC5 — dry-run mode"
echo "======================================================="

run_act_for_test_case \
    "TC5: dry-run mode" \
    "tc5" \
    "$SCRIPT_DIR/tests/fixtures/test_dry_run.csv"

assert_contains "TC5" "DRY-RUN MODE"                          "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC5" "DELETE old-artifact max_age 1048576"   "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC5" "RETAIN new-artifact"                    "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC5" "space_reclaimed=1048576"                "$CASE_LOG"
assert_contains "TC5" "Job succeeded"                          "$CASE_LOG"
rm -rf "$CASE_TMPDIR"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Final summary
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
echo ""
echo "======================================================="
echo " RESULTS"
echo "======================================================="
echo "  Passed: $PASS_COUNT"
echo "  Failed: $FAIL_COUNT"
echo ""
echo "  act-result.txt written to: $ACT_RESULT"

{
    echo ""
    echo "################################################################"
    echo "# HARNESS SUMMARY"
    echo "# Passed: $PASS_COUNT"
    echo "# Failed: $FAIL_COUNT"
    echo "################################################################"
} >> "$ACT_RESULT"

if [[ "$FAIL_COUNT" -gt 0 ]]; then
    echo "HARNESS FAILED: $FAIL_COUNT assertion(s) failed" >&2
    exit 1
fi

echo "All assertions passed."
exit 0
